assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains spot instances have preemption characteristics and discusses how workloads handle interruption gracefully, relating this to the upgrade drain process

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Workloads are preemption-tolerant by design — your applications already handle interruption gracefully' and explains this makes them lower risk for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends 5% surge for spot pools (higher than the 1-node default for on-demand) and explains 'you can be more aggressive with surge settings' due to fault tolerance

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends maxUnavailable=2 for spot pools and explains 'Spot workloads tolerate node loss, so draining 2 nodes simultaneously is acceptable'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the risk that surge nodes might not be available due to spot capacity constraints or that new spot instances are not guaranteed during upgrade

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes a section 'Still configure PDBs for spot workloads' with example YAML and explains PDBs ensure 'orderly drain during upgrades rather than simultaneous eviction'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands for updating node pools with surge settings: 'gcloud container node-pools update SPOT_POOL_NAME --max-surge-upgrade 5% --max-unavailable-upgrade 2'

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Upgrade spot pools FIRST, then on-demand pools' and provides a detailed sequencing strategy with Phase 1 for spot pools and Phase 2 for on-demand pools
